package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "github.com/gorilla/websocket"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for websocket_without_origin_check: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: WebSocket upgrader without origin validation, allowing cross-site WebSocket hijacking.
type CaseWebsocketWithoutOriginCheckDTO struct {
    ID       string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status   string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount   int    `json:"amount"`
    Payload  []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseWebsocketWithoutOriginCheckModel struct {
    ID        string       `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID  string       `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status    string       `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseWebsocketWithoutOriginCheckRepository struct {
    db    *gorm.DB
    sql   *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseWebsocketWithoutOriginCheckModel
}

type CaseWebsocketWithoutOriginCheckService struct {
    repo   *CaseWebsocketWithoutOriginCheckRepository
    client *http.Client
}

type CaseWebsocketWithoutOriginCheckAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

var upgraderNoCheck = websocket.Upgrader{
    CheckOrigin: func(r *http.Request) bool { return true },
}

func PositiveWebsocketWithoutOriginCheck(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseWebsocketWithoutOriginCheckDTO) error {
    // Upgrader accepts ALL origins — cross-site WebSocket hijacking risk.
    conn, err := upgraderNoCheck.Upgrade(c.Writer, c.Request, nil)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer conn.Close()
    _ = conn.WriteMessage(websocket.TextMessage, []byte(input))
    return nil
}
